name: Boston Trifle
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 300°F (150°C) and position the rack in the lower third.
2. Pour the cake flour and 7 tablespoons of granulated sugar into a sifter or sieve, then sift onto a sheet of waxed paper. Set aside.
3. Measure the remaining 7 tablespoons and 1 tablespoon of granulated sugar; set aside.
4. Separate the eggs: place whites in a heavy-duty mixer bowl and yolks in a 1 1/2 quart bowl.
5. Whip egg yolks on high speed for 3 to 5 minutes, adding vanilla and continuing to whip for 15 seconds. The mixture should be thicker, pale yellow, and increased in volume.
6. Test the consistency: lift some of the mixture with beaters; if it forms ribbons that slowly dissolve on the surface, proceed to whipping the whites.
7. If the test passes, attach the bowl of whites to a mixer with the whisk attachment. Whip on medium-low speed for 30 to 45 seconds until soft peaks form.
8. Add cream of tartar and 1 tablespoon granulated sugar; continue whipping until glossy and stiff but not dry or grainy.
9. Gently fold the egg yolk mixture into the whipped whites using a rubber spatula, incorporating half of the sugar at a time.
10. Sprinkle flour over the egg mixture and fold to incorporate.
11. Pour batter into an ungreased 9-inch springform pan; smooth the surface.
12. Bake for 55 to 60 minutes until golden and spongy, testing with a toothpick in the center (should come out clean).
13. Remove from oven, turn upside down, and cool for 2 hours before turning right side up.
14. Release the cake from the pan by loosening the crust, tapping gently on the counter, and rotating until the removable bottom comes off.
15. Chocolate Butter Glaze:
- Chop chocolate into matchstick pieces.
- Melt butter in a double boiler; add chopped chocolate until smooth.
16. Trifle Custard Filling:
- Whisk egg yolks with sugar, then stir into warm half and half.
- Add flour and whisk to combine; bring to boil and simmer.
- Cool, cover surface with plastic wrap, and whip heavy cream to Chantilly stage; fold in.
17. Assemble: divide spongecake into three layers, spread custard between each layer, top with remaining layer, chill for up to 1-2 hours before serving.
18. Drizzle glaze over the dessert and sprinkle chocolate flakes around the rim. Refrigerate before serving.
